verdict.answer_first: A starts with an immediate, time-boxed action plan ('Phase 0 — Stop the bleeding (today, ~15 min)'), while B starts by explaining what it can't do ('I can't execute any of that from this session').
verdict.decisiveness: A commits to a full plan based on sensible defaults ('I'll go with sensible defaults and you can correct any that don't fit'), while B stops and asks for instructions ('tell me how you want to grant that access and I'll start there').
verdict.actionability: A provides concrete CLI commands to run immediately, while B provides a meta-discussion about needing access before it can provide any commands.
